evals.prompt: We signed a 5-year office lease: 10,000,000 NGN per year paid annually in arrears, IBR 18%, no incentives, no restoration. Compute the initial lease liability and ROU asset and give me year-1 entries.
evals.expected_output: Liability = PV of 5 payments of 10M at 18% = ~31,272,000 NGN. ROU = same. Year 1: interest = ~5,629,000, payment 10M reduces liability to ~26,901,000; ROU depreciation = ~6,254,400/yr straight-line.
assertions: Computes initial liability of approximately 31.27 million NGN using PV at 18%
Sets ROU asset equal to the liability absent other adjustments
Year-1 interest of approximately 5.63 million and closing liability of approximately 26.9 million
Straight-line ROU depreciation of approximately 6.25 million per year
Performs the calculation with code, not mental math

evals.prompt: Our logistics contract gives us exclusive use of 10 identified trucks; the provider can swap trucks only if one breaks down, and we decide routes and cargo. Is this a lease?
evals.expected_output: Yes — identified assets (substitution right not substantive, only on breakdown), customer directs use and obtains substantially all benefits. Embedded lease in a service contract; separate lease and non-lease components or apply the practical expedient.
assertions: Concludes the arrangement contains a lease
Explains why the substitution right is not substantive
Applies the control test (direct use + economic benefits)
Addresses separation of lease vs. service components

evals.prompt: Three years into a 10-year lease, we negotiated to cut the leased floor space by half, with rent halved too. How do we account for it?
evals.expected_output: Partial termination/scope decrease: reduce ROU proportionately (50%), reduce liability via remeasurement with a revised discount rate, recognize the difference between liability reduction and ROU reduction as gain/loss in P&L.
assertions: Identifies this as a modification reducing scope, not a separate lease
Reduces the ROU asset proportionate to the scope decrease
Remeasures the liability using a revised discount rate at modification date
Recognizes resulting gain or loss in P&L

evals.prompt: Using the attached lease portfolio (evals/files/lease_portfolio.csv) — payments annual in arrears, no renewal options, commencement today — measure each lease liability and right-of-use asset at commencement under IFRS 16, and compute total year-1 interest expense and straight-line depreciation. Produce a commencement summary table and the initial journal entry.
evals.expected_output: Liabilities: L-01 $1,025,049, L-02 $689,597, L-03 $194,383; total $1,909,029 (ROU assets equal liabilities here). Year-1 interest $144,416; year-1 depreciation $339,805.
assertions: L-01 lease liability approx $1,025,049
L-02 lease liability approx $689,597
L-03 lease liability approx $194,383
Total liability and ROU approx $1,909,029
Year-1 interest expense approx $144,416
Year-1 depreciation approx $339,805
Computes present values with code, not approximation

evals.prompt: [adversarial] Measure the commencement lease liability for the lease in the attached file (evals/files/lease_advance.csv). Watch the payment timing.
evals.expected_output: Payments are in ADVANCE (annuity-due): liability 998,542, NOT the arrears figure 924,576. (Under IFRS 16 the day-one payment is often shown as reducing the liability immediately or added to ROU - either presentation accepted if the annuity-due math is right.)
assertions: Uses annuity-due treatment for in-advance payments (PV approx 998,542, or equivalently 924,576 liability plus the first payment in ROU - either is acceptable if explained)
Does not present the plain arrears figure 924,576 as the answer without addressing timing
Explicitly notes the timing field in the data
Computes with code
